name: Google Scholar Search
description: Search Google Scholar and extract paper-shaped results: title, authors, snippet, paper URL, and a link to the cited-by page.

browser_automation.description: Hits scholar.google.com directly. Selectors target the gs_r card layout used for academic results.

sequences.name: google_scholar_search_extract
sequences.description: Run a Scholar query and extract paper cards.

required_variables.name: search_query
required_variables.description: Academic search query. Aliases: query, q.

steps.name: Wait for the page to settle

help.summary: Search Google Scholar and extract paper title/authors/snippet/url + cited-by link.

parameters.name: search_query
parameters.description: Academic search query. Aliases query and q work too.

examples.command: rzn-browser run google scholar --param search_query="transformer attention mechanism"
rzn-browser run google scholar --param query="author:hinton deep learning"

help.returns: Array of { title, url, authors, snippet, cited_by_url }. authors is the raw 'authors — venue, year — site' line; parse downstream if you need fields.
notes: Scholar aggressively rate-limits scraping; expect intermittent 429s on busy sessions.
url is the paper's primary link (often direct PDF or publisher page), not a Scholar-internal link.
